title: Tangy Velvety American Chicken Noodles
description: A sour and velvety American dish featuring yuzu-kosho, perfect for a monsoon evening, made with halal-friendly ingredients.
instructions: Bring the brine to a boil in a large pot, then reduce the heat to medium-low and add the chicken breast pieces.
Cook the chicken for 10 minutes or until it reaches an internal temperature of 165 degrees.
While the chicken is cooking, cook the noodles according to the package instructions.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and add the minced garlic and grated ginger.
Cook the garlic and ginger for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the yuzu-kosho to the skillet and stir to combine with the garlic and ginger.
Once the chicken is cooked, remove it from the brine and add it to the skillet with the yuzu-kosho mixture.
Stir to combine the chicken with the yuzu-kosho mixture and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the cooked noodles to the skillet and stir to combine with the chicken and yuzu-kosho mixture.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the Tangy Velvety American Chicken Noodles hot and enjoy.
